snp4.3:1.1: There are some who dispute corrupted at heart,
snp4.3:1.2: and those who dispute their hearts set on truth,
snp4.3:1.3: but a sage doesn’t enter a dispute that’s arisen,
snp4.3:1.4: which is why he has no rigidity anywhere at all.
snp4.3:2.2: led on by desire, entrenched in his likes,
snp4.3:2.3: forming his own conclusions, overcome his own views?
snp4.3:2.4: He’d dispute in line with the way that he knows.
snp4.3:3.3: is, say the skilled, ignoble by nature—
snp4.3:3.4: he who speaks of himself of his own accord.
snp4.3:4.1: But a monk at peace, fully unbound in himself,
snp4.3:4.2: not boasting of his habits —”That’s how I am”—
snp4.3:4.3: he, say the skilled, is noble by nature—
snp4.3:4.4: he with no vanity anywhere in the world.
snp4.3:5.3: when he sees it to his own advantage—
snp4.3:5.4: relies on a peace dependent on the provoked.
snp4.3:6.1: Because entrenchments in views aren’t easily overcome
snp4.3:6.2: when considering what’s grasped among doctrines,
snp4.3:6.3: that’s why a person embraces or rejects a doctrine—
snp4.3:6.4: in light of these very entrenchments.
snp4.3:7.1: Now, one who is cleansed has no theorized view
snp4.3:7.2: about states of becoming or not- anywhere in the world.
snp4.3:7.4:
by what means would he go?
He isn’t involved,
snp4.3:8.1: for one who’s involved enters into disputes over doctrines.
snp4.3:8.2: But how—in connection with what— would you argue with one uninvolved?
snp4.3:8.3: He has nothing embraced or rejected,
snp4.3:8.4: has sloughed off every view right here—every one.
